title: Tech Stack
description: Technical repertoire organized as a radar of tools, fundamentals, and capabilities I track to make better decisions.
hint: Scroll to navigate the technical radar and follow the progression of the stack.
introTitle: Experience shows where I applied it. This map shows the repertoire I keep in motion.
introBody: Not every tool carries the same weight: some are daily foundations, others help me prototype, integrate, or respond when the context changes.
outroTitle: A stack is not a logo collection. It is repertoire for choosing with more precision.
outroBody: The stronger signal is connecting tools, fundamentals, and current practices without treating novelty as an automatic answer.

opening.title: The map separates real use, foundations, and technologies I follow closely.
opening.body: The experience sections show where I have applied the work. Here the goal is technical range, current awareness, and how I connect tools without pretending every item has the same depth.

languages.title: Languages and runtimes that cover product, services, automation, and data.
languages.body: This foundation supports day-to-day delivery: building interfaces, services, scripts, integrations, and queries with clarity about where each rule should live.

architectures.title: Architecture organizes boundaries, integration, and change before the tool choice.
architectures.body: These foundations help read the domain, split responsibilities, reduce coupling, and keep evolution possible when the product changes.

devops.title: Cloud, containers, and pipelines turn delivery into a repeatable capability.
devops.body: This repertoire matters when software has to leave the local machine with less fragile environments, releases, automation, and execution.

ai.title: AI enters as product capability, automation, and context, not decoration.
ai.body: LLMs, RAG, MCP, and agents stay on the radar because they are already changing retrieval, orchestration, and assistance flows. The criterion is still verifiable utility.

extras.title: Frameworks, tooling, and quality close the loop between DX and final experience.
extras.body: This layer keeps delivery faster to validate, easier to maintain, and more consistent for the people building and using the product.

finale.title: This radar shows technical range without turning every name into a claim of specialty.
finale.body: Some tools are delivery foundations; others expand the repertoire for deciding, prototyping, or integrating better when the problem asks for it.

stage.ariaLabel: Narrative stack visualization driven by scroll

languages.title: Languages
languages.summary: Use with confidence across applications, services, automation, data, and integrations.

architectures.title: Architectures
architectures.summary: Foundations that travel across stacks, domain logic, integration, and responsibility boundaries.

devops.title: DevOps
devops.summary: Operate in product context with infrastructure, pipelines, and automation.

ai.title: AI
ai.summary: Technologies I track and apply when they increase context or reduce friction.

extras.title: Extras
extras.summary: Delivery refinement across DX, performance, validation, and final experience.
